summary: The documentation build failed because `transformers/modeling_utils.py` contains an unresolved Git merge conflict marker (`<<<<<<< sonic-moe`) at line 1974, which causes a Python `SyntaxError`. The doc-builder triggered this when trying to import the `llama` model module while processing `model_doc/llama.md`.
root_cause: An unresolved Git merge conflict marker (`<<<<<<< sonic-moe`) was left in `transformers/modeling_utils.py` at line 1974, making the file invalid Python syntax and crashing any import that transitively loads it.

evidence.reason: Explicitly shows a Git merge conflict marker at line 1974 of modeling_utils.py, which Python cannot parse.
Python raises a SyntaxError because the conflict marker `<<<<<<< sonic-moe` is not valid Python syntax.

evidence.quote:
SyntaxError: There was an error when converting ../transformers/docs/source/en/model_doc/llama.md to the MDX format.
invalid syntax
evidence.reason: Confirms the doc-builder propagated the SyntaxError as a fatal failure while processing the llama documentation page.
The unhandled SyntaxError caused the doc-builder process to exit with a non-zero code, failing the CI job.

suggested_fix: Resolve the merge conflict in `transformers/modeling_utils.py` around line 1974 by removing the conflict markers (`<<<<<<< sonic-moe`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>`) and keeping only the correct version of the code, then re-push the branch.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
